summary: The most likely diagnosis is herpes zoster (zoster). This condition arises when the latent varicella-zoster virus becomes active again after years of dormancy, especially in older adults who have weakened immune systems. It presents as painful skin lesions that follow specific dermatomes, often affecting areas like the thoracic region where this patient reports symptoms.
